records.ety: [L. connatus; con- + natus born, p. p. of nasci. See Cognate.]

defs: Born with another; being of the same birth.

quotes: A difference has been made by some; those diseases or conditions which are dependent on original conformation being called congenital; while the diseases of affections that may have supervened during gestation or delivery are called connate.

defs: Congenitally united; growing from one base, or united at their bases; united into one body; as, connate leaves or athers. See Illust. of Connate-perfoliate.
